name: sbd
title: sbd

commands.name: sbd

arguments.raw: -e prog     program to execute after connect (e.g. -e cmd.exe or -e bash)

arguments.description: infinitely respawn/reconnect, pause for n seconds between
arguments.raw: -r n        infinitely respawn/reconnect, pause for n seconds between

arguments.description: encryption on/off. specify whether you want to use the built-in
arguments.raw: -c on|off   encryption on/off. specify whether you want to use the built-in

arguments.default: phrase to use for encryption (secret must be
arguments.raw: -k secret   override default phrase to use for encryption (secret must be

arguments.description: toggle numeric-only IP addresses (don't do DNS resolution). if
arguments.raw: -n          toggle numeric-only IP addresses (don't do DNS resolution). if

arguments.description: toggle monitoring (snooping) on/off (only used with the -e
arguments.raw: -m          toggle monitoring (snooping) on/off (only used with the -e

arguments.description: add prefix (+ a hardcoded separator) to all outbound data.
arguments.raw: -P prefix   add prefix (+ a hardcoded separator) to all outbound data.

arguments.description: highlight incoming data with a hardcoded (color) escape
arguments.raw: -H on|off   highlight incoming data with a hardcoded (color) escape

arguments.description: print version banner and exit (include that output in your
arguments.raw: -V          print version banner and exit (include that output in your

arguments.description: invoke a shell, nothing else. if sbd is setuid 0, it'll invoke
arguments.raw: -s          invoke a shell, nothing else. if sbd is setuid 0, it'll invoke

arguments.description: "immobility timeout" in seconds for idle read/write operations
arguments.raw: -w n        "immobility timeout" in seconds for idle read/write operations

arguments.description: fork and run in background (daemonize). default: -D off
arguments.raw: -D on|off   fork and run in background (daemonize). default: -D off

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# sbd -h
 sbd 1.37 Copyright (C) 2004 Michel Blomgren <michel.blomgren@tigerteam.se>
 $Id: sbd.c,v 1.37 2005/08/21 22:40:47 shadow Exp $
 
 This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under
 the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free Software
 Foundation; either version 2 of the License, or (at your option) any later
 version.
 
 connect (tcp): sbd [-options] host port
 listen (tcp):  sbd -l -p port [-options]
 options:
   -l          listen for incoming connection
   -p n        choose port to listen on, or source port to connect out from
   -a address  choose an address to listen on or connect out from
   -e prog     program to execute after connect (e.g. -e cmd.exe or -e bash)
   -r n        infinitely respawn/reconnect, pause for n seconds between
               connection attempts. -r0 can be used to re-listen after
               disconnect (just like a regular daemon)
   -c on|off   encryption on/off. specify whether you want to use the built-in
               AES-CBC-128 + HMAC-SHA1 encryption implementation (by
               Christophe Devine - http://www.cr0.net:8040/) or not
               default is: -c on
   -k secret   override default phrase to use for encryption (secret must be
               shared between client and server)
   -q          hush, quiet, don't print anything (overrides -v)
   -v          be verbose
   -n          toggle numeric-only IP addresses (don't do DNS resolution). if
               you specify -n twice, original state will be active (i.e. -n
               works like a on/off switch)
   -m          toggle monitoring (snooping) on/off (only used with the -e
               option). snooping can also be turned on by specifying -vv (-v
               two times)
   -P prefix   add prefix (+ a hardcoded separator) to all outbound data.
               this option is mostly only useful for sbd in "chat mode" (to
               prefix lines you send with your nickname)
   -H on|off   highlight incoming data with a hardcoded (color) escape
               sequence (for e.g. chatting). default is: -H off
   -V          print version banner and exit (include that output in your
               bug report and send bug report to michel.blomgren@tigerteam.se)
 unix-like OS specific options:
   -s          invoke a shell, nothing else. if sbd is setuid 0, it'll invoke
               a root shell
   -w n        "immobility timeout" in seconds for idle read/write operations
               and program execution (the -e option)
   -D on|off   fork and run in background (daemonize). default: -D off

local_help.-h:
sbd 1.37 Copyright (C) 2004 Michel Blomgren <michel.blomgren@tigerteam.se>
$Id: sbd.c,v 1.37 2005/08/21 22:40:47 shadow Exp $

This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under
the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free Software
Foundation; either version 2 of the License, or (at your option) any later
version.

connect (tcp): sbd [-options] host port
listen (tcp):  sbd -l -p port [-options]
options:
    -l          listen for incoming connection
    -p n        choose port to listen on, or source port to connect out from
    -a address  choose an address to listen on or connect out from
    -e prog     program to execute after connect (e.g. -e cmd.exe or -e bash)
    -r n        infinitely respawn/reconnect, pause for n seconds between
                connection attempts. -r0 can be used to re-listen after
                disconnect (just like a regular daemon)
    -c on|off   encryption on/off. specify whether you want to use the built-in
                AES-CBC-128 + HMAC-SHA1 encryption implementation (by
                Christophe Devine - http://www.cr0.net:8040/) or not
                default is: -c on
    -k secret   override default phrase to use for encryption (secret must be
                shared between client and server)
    -q          hush, quiet, don't print anything (overrides -v)
    -v          be verbose
    -n          toggle numeric-only IP addresses (don't do DNS resolution). if
                you specify -n twice, original state will be active (i.e. -n
                works like a on/off switch)
    -m          toggle monitoring (snooping) on/off (only used with the -e
                option). snooping can also be turned on by specifying -vv (-v
                two times)
    -P prefix   add prefix (+ a hardcoded separator) to all outbound data.
                this option is mostly only useful for sbd in "chat mode" (to
                prefix lines you send with your nickname)
    -H on|off   highlight incoming data with a hardcoded (color) escape
                sequence (for e.g. chatting). default is: -H off
    -V          print version banner and exit (include that output in your
                bug report and send bug report to michel.blomgren@tigerteam.se)
unix-like OS specific options:
    -s          invoke a shell, nothing else. if sbd is setuid 0, it'll invoke
                a root shell
    -w n        "immobility timeout" in seconds for idle read/write operations
                and program execution (the -e option)
    -D on|off   fork and run in background (daemonize). default: -D off
local_help.help: no port to connect to (-h for help)

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=a17ce6ccdd83df1c40a7ea746f7d8b427a9d904b, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
Secure backdoor for Linux and Windows
sbd is a Netcat-clone, designed to be portable and offer strong encryption. It
runs on Unix-like operating systems and on Microsoft Win32. sbd features
AES-CBC-128 + HMAC-SHA1 encryption (by Christophe Devine), program execution
(-e option), choosing source port, continuous reconnection with delay, and
some other nice features. sbd supports TCP/IP communication only.
